# rom.rom_error_est_t { #skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t }

`rom.rom_error_est_t`

Compute and visualize ROM error metrics for flat (vectorized) data reconstructions.

This module provides:
  - compute_rom_error_metrics_flat: calculates time-dependent and global error measures
    (L2, L∞, RMSE, MAE, R², explained variance, quantiles, and optional energy norm).
  - plot_rom_error_diagnostics_flat: diagnostic plots including true vs. ROM scatter,
    spatial snapshots for selected snapshots, and raincloud plots of error and speed-up.
  - generate_rom_error_report: prints a structured summary of global and time-dependent
    ROM error statistics to the console.

## Notes on shapes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes-on-shapes}

Supported shapes for u and u_rom:
  - (n_snapshots, n_space)
  - (n_snapshots, n_time, n_space)

If your data uses a different axis order, pass snapshot_axis/time_axis/space_axis.

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [compute_rom_error_metrics_flat](#skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.compute_rom_error_metrics_flat) | Compute various error metrics between full-order and ROM reconstructions. |
| [generate_rom_error_report](#skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.generate_rom_error_report) | Print a structured summary of ROM error metrics. |
| [plot_rom_param_diagnostics_t](#skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.plot_rom_param_diagnostics_t) | u, u_rom: (p, t, dof) |

### compute_rom_error_metrics_flat { #skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.compute_rom_error_metrics_flat }

```python
rom.rom_error_est_t.compute_rom_error_metrics_flat(
    u,
    u_rom,
    K=None,
    *,
    snapshot_axis=0,
    time_axis=None,
    space_axis=-1,
    eps=1e-30,
)
```

Compute various error metrics between full-order and ROM reconstructions.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type       | Description                                                                                                                                            | Default    |
|---------------|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| u             |            | Full-order and ROM fields. Supported shapes:   - (n_snapshots, n_space)   - (n_snapshots, n_time, n_space)                                             | _required_ |
| u_rom         |            | Full-order and ROM fields. Supported shapes:   - (n_snapshots, n_space)   - (n_snapshots, n_time, n_space)                                             | _required_ |
| K             | array_like | Stiffness matrix for energy-norm metrics. Must be (n_space, n_space). If provided and u is 3D, the energy terms are accumulated over (snapshot, time). | `None`     |
| snapshot_axis | int        | Axis indices used if u has a different ordering. For 2D arrays, keep time_axis=None.                                                                   | `0`        |
| time_axis     | int        | Axis indices used if u has a different ordering. For 2D arrays, keep time_axis=None.                                                                   | `0`        |
| space_axis    | int        | Axis indices used if u has a different ordering. For 2D arrays, keep time_axis=None.                                                                   | `0`        |
| eps           | float      | Small number to avoid division-by-zero.                                                                                                                | `1e-30`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name    | Type   | Description                                                                                                        |
|---------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| metrics | dict   | Contains time-dependent and global metrics. For 3D inputs, time-dependent arrays have shape (n_snapshots, n_time). |

### generate_rom_error_report { #skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.generate_rom_error_report }

```python
rom.rom_error_est_t.generate_rom_error_report(
    metrics,
    name='ROM Accuracy Report',
)
```

Print a structured summary of ROM error metrics.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name    | Type   | Description                                                   | Default                 |
|---------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| metrics | dict   | Error metrics dictionary from compute_rom_error_metrics_flat. | _required_              |
| name    | str    | Title of the report.                                          | `'ROM Accuracy Report'` |

### plot_rom_param_diagnostics_t { #skrom.rom.rom_error_est_t.plot_rom_param_diagnostics_t }

```python
rom.rom_error_est_t.plot_rom_param_diagnostics_t(
    u,
    u_rom,
    *,
    l2_per_param=None,
    speedup_per_param=None,
    param_labels=None,
    eps=1e-16,
    log_error=True,
    use_raincloud=True,
)
```

u, u_rom: (p, t, dof)

Plots:
  1) per-parameter aggregated relative error (bar)
  2) per-parameter per-timestep error (boxplot)
  3) error vs speed-up (scatter) if speedup given
  4) L2 norms (bar) if l2 given
  5) raincloud: rel_err_p distribution (across parameters)
  6) raincloud: rel_err_pt grouped by parameter (across timesteps)
  7) raincloud: speedup distribution (across parameters) if speedup given
